the_signal.headline: Agent-to-Agent protocols move from theory to production spec
the_signal.body: n8n published a production-grade A2A protocol spec covering failure modes and enterprise orchestration—this is the standardization layer that turns multi-agent chaos into debuggable systems. For GTM orgs running 3+ agents in revenue workflows, this means you can finally stop building custom inter-agent communication and start using battle-tested patterns. The real signal: infrastructure is catching up to the agent explosion.

tool_drop.name: AgentPeek
tool_drop.what: Debugging and visibility layer purpose-built for multi-agent workflows
tool_drop.why: You can't optimize what you can't see—this directly addresses the multi-agent debugging blocker that's currently halting GTM teams from scaling beyond single-agent pilots

thread_of_day.title: Production AI Playbook: Complex Agent Patterns
thread_of_day.takeaway: Memory management and sub-workflow composition are non-negotiable once you move past proof-of-concept—teams scaling agents are hitting the same architectural walls and need patterns, not more frameworks

quote.text: When a review run falls short, this workflow catches it and proposes a fix to the skill, then writes the improved instructions back once you approve the diff.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Build Self-Improving Agent Skills

whats_moving.context: Enterprise orchestration requires formal specification—the vacuum is closing and custom integration debt is about to become obsolete
Mixture-of-Experts models are proving you don't need monolithic inference—sparse activation reduces cost per token significantly for routing-heavy GTM stacks
Agents that audit their own output and request instruction updates are moving from labs into production—this closes the feedback loop that manual iteration can't handle at scale

act_on_this: Map your current multi-agent workflows against the A2A protocol spec—identify which inter-agent communication patterns you're building custom and flag them for replacement when tools stabilize around the emerging standard in Q3.

numbers.stat: 48B active parameters in LongCat-2.0 (1.6T total MoE)
numbers.context: This efficiency level makes distributed agent inference viable for GTM teams without enterprise GPU budgets—smaller active models mean faster response times and lower per-token cost at scale
